input_payload: {"direction": "How does training data size affect cross-institutional generalization in CNN-based pneumonia detection from chest radiographs?", "lit_digest": {"summary": "CheXNet (Rajpurkar 2017) achieved AUROC 0.7680 on NIH ChestX-ray14 pneumonia. Subsequent work (Zech 2018, Pooch 2019) showed substantial AUROC drops on external sites due to confounders like scanner brand and image-acquisition protocol.", "key_works": [{"title": "CheXNet: Radiologist-Level Pneumonia Detection on Chest X-Rays", "authors": ["Rajpurkar et al."], "year": 2017, "doi_or_arxiv": "arxiv:1711.05225", "relevance": "high"}, {"title": "Variable generalization performance of a deep learning model to detect pneumonia in chest radiographs", "authors": ["Zech et al."], "year": 2018, "doi_or_arxiv": "10.1371/journal.pmed.1002683", "relevance": "high"}], "gaps": ["No systematic study of how training-set size mediates the in-domain vs external AUROC gap", "Existing work tests on one external dataset; no two-site external validation"], "consensus": ["In-domain AUROC ~0.80-0.85 with 100k images"], "disagreements": ["External AUROC range varies wildly across studies"], "tools_needed": false}}
expected_summary: Five concrete, methodologically specific ideas that target the named gap (size vs. cross-institutional gap). Each idea must cite at least one literature work by name, declare feasibility on a 5090, name specific datasets (NIH/PadChest/CheXpert from the digest), and offer falsifiable failure modes (e.g., 'PadChest scanner mix may compress effect by >0.05 AUROC'). At least one idea should be deliberately ambitious. No 'use a transformer' pseudo-novelty.
notes: Strong literature digest available; the idea_gen agent should produce grounding=strong on every idea. Failure modes that name a specific confounder (scanner brand, acquisition protocol) score higher than generic ones.
